text:
Ramp is seeking a talented Frontend Engineer to join our team in New York, NY on a Contract basis. We're looking for someone who can help us build and refine the user-facing experiences that power our platform. This is an excellent opportunity to work with modern technologies and contribute meaningfully to a fast-growing fintech company.

In this role, you'll be responsible for developing and maintaining frontend components using React and other contemporary frameworks. You'll collaborate closely with our design and backend teams to translate product requirements into intuitive interfaces that our users love. Your day-to-day work will include writing clean, maintainable code, participating in code reviews, debugging complex issues, and optimizing application performance. You'll also contribute to our component library, ensuring consistency across our platform and enabling other engineers to build features more efficiently.

We're looking for someone with strong JavaScript fundamentals and demonstrable experience building scalable frontend applications. You should have at least three years of professional experience and be comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where priorities shift and ambiguity is common. Experience with TypeScript, state management solutions like Redux or MobX, and testing frameworks is highly valued. Beyond technical skills, we need someone who communicates clearly, takes ownership of their work, and genuinely cares about user experience.

The compensation for this position is competitive, reflecting the market rate for skilled frontend engineers in the New York area. While this is a contract position, it offers flexibility and the chance to work on meaningful problems. You'll have access to our standard contractor benefits and will be integrated fully into our engineering team.

You should be comfortable in a collaborative environment and excited about the opportunity to shape how businesses manage their finances. If you're passionate about frontend development, enjoy solving complex problems, and want to contribute to a company that's transforming how companies handle spend management, we'd love to hear from you. Please submit your resume along with links to your portfolio or GitHub profile so we can review your work.

Cloudflare is seeking a talented Platform Engineer Internship candidate to join our London office on a Hybrid basis. This is an exciting opportunity to work with a team that builds and maintains the infrastructure powering a more secure and faster internet for millions of users worldwide.

In this role, you will contribute to the design, development, and operation of our platform systems that handle massive scale and reliability requirements. You'll work alongside experienced engineers to improve our internal tooling, automate operational tasks, and help build the systems that keep our global network running smoothly. Your responsibilities will include developing automation scripts and tools, assisting with infrastructure improvements, troubleshooting platform issues, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks. You may also participate in on-call rotations and contribute to our incident response processes, gaining real-world experience in production systems management.

We're looking for someone with a strong foundation in computer science fundamentals and experience with at least one programming language such as Python, Go, or Rust. Familiarity with cloud platforms, containerization technologies like Docker or Kubernetes, and basic networking concepts is highly valuable. You should demonstrate problem-solving skills, the ability to learn quickly, and genuine enthusiasm for building reliable systems. While not required, experience with infrastructure-as-code tools or exposure to distributed systems is a plus.

Cloudflare offers interns a genuine opportunity to make meaningful contributions while developing professional skills. You'll have access to mentorship from experienced engineers, exposure to real production systems, and the chance to work on projects that impact our global infrastructure. We provide a collaborative working environment where your ideas are heard and valued. Our hybrid setup in London allows you to work flexibly, combining in-office collaboration with remote work to suit your productivity style.

We believe diverse perspectives strengthen our engineering, and we're committed to building an inclusive workplace. We encourage applications from underrepresented groups in technology and provide equal opportunities regardless of background, experience level, or identity. This internship offers not just technical growth but also insight into how modern internet infrastructure operates at scale, making it an ideal launching point for your engineering career.

Databricks is seeking a talented Platform Engineer for an Internship position based in San Francisco, CA. This is an excellent opportunity to work with cutting-edge data and AI technologies while contributing to products used by thousands of organizations worldwide. You'll join our engineering team during a pivotal time as we continue to scale our platform and expand our capabilities in the data intelligence space.

In this role, you'll be responsible for developing and maintaining core infrastructure components that power the Databricks platform. You'll work on building reliable, scalable systems that handle massive data processing workloads, implementing monitoring and observability solutions, and collaborating with senior engineers to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks. Your contributions will directly impact the stability and efficiency of our platform, touching code that runs in production environments serving our largest customers. You may also participate in code reviews, help troubleshoot production incidents, and contribute to technical documentation that helps the team maintain high engineering standards.

We're looking for someone with strong fundamentals in computer science and software engineering. You should have solid experience with at least one programming language such as Python, Java, or Go, and demonstrate understanding of distributed systems concepts. Familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS or GCP is a plus, as is experience with containerization technologies or Kubernetes. We value problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to communicate clearly with team members. While we prefer candidates who have completed coursework in operating systems, networking, or databases, the most important qualities are curiosity, initiative, and willingness to learn from experienced engineers.

Databricks offers competitive benefits including flexible work arrangements, comprehensive health coverage, and professional development opportunities. The estimated salary range for this internship position is 90,000 - 120,000, depending on experience and qualifications. You'll have access to our learning resources, mentorship from senior engineers, and exposure to real-world challenges in data infrastructure. This internship can be a springboard for a full-time role at Databricks. If you're passionate about building the systems that power data science and ready to make an impact, we'd love to hear from you.

Sentry is looking for a Site Reliability Engineer to join our growing team in London, UK. We're seeking someone passionate about building reliable, scalable systems that help developers solve problems faster. This is a full-time position where you'll have the opportunity to work on infrastructure and systems that support thousands of companies worldwide.

In this role, you'll be responsible for designing and maintaining the systems that keep our platform running smoothly around the clock. You'll work on improving our deployment pipelines, optimizing database performance, and building monitoring solutions that catch issues before they impact users. Your work will directly influence how our platform scales as we grow, and you'll collaborate closely with both our engineering and product teams to ensure reliability is built into everything we do.

We're looking for someone with strong fundamentals in Linux systems, networking, and databases. You should have hands-on experience with cloud platforms like AWS or GCP, and be comfortable working with containerization technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes. Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools and CI/CD pipelines is essential. We value someone who not only solves immediate problems but thinks strategically about system architecture and long-term scalability. Strong communication skills are important too—you'll be working across teams and documenting complex systems for others to understand.

Your background should include at least three years of SRE, DevOps, or similar infrastructure engineering experience. You should be comfortable with on-call rotations and have experience responding to and learning from incidents. Python or Go experience is a plus, though we're more interested in your problem-solving approach than specific languages.

We offer a competitive benefits package including comprehensive health coverage, professional development opportunities, and flexible working arrangements. You'll have access to the latest tools and technology, and work in a collaborative environment where your ideas are valued. We're committed to building a diverse team and creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can do their best work.

If you're excited about tackling infrastructure challenges and helping developers build better software, we'd love to hear from you.

Figma is looking for a Full-Stack Engineer to join our San Francisco, CA office on a contract basis. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced engineer who wants to make an immediate impact on our design platform while maintaining flexibility in their work arrangement.

In this role, you'll work across our entire technology stack, contributing to both frontend and backend systems that power Figma's collaborative design tools. You'll collaborate with product managers, designers, and other engineers to build features that millions of users rely on daily. Your responsibilities will include architecting scalable services, optimizing performance across our web and desktop applications, and ensuring our infrastructure can handle real-time collaboration at scale. You'll also participate in code reviews, contribute to our technical documentation, and help mentor other team members as needed.

We're seeking someone with strong full-stack capabilities and at least five years of professional software engineering experience. You should be comfortable working with modern web technologies, have a solid understanding of database design and API architecture, and demonstrate proficiency in at least one backend language such as Go, Python, or Rust. Experience with React or similar frontend frameworks is important, as is familiarity with cloud infrastructure and containerization. Beyond technical skills, we value clear communication, a collaborative mindset, and someone who cares deeply about code quality and user experience.

This contract position offers a competitive salary of $150,000 - $200,000, depending on experience and background. You'll have access to Figma's comprehensive benefits package, including health insurance, professional development opportunities, and the flexibility that comes with contract work. You'll also benefit from working within a talented engineering organization that values innovation, moves fast, and maintains a strong culture of learning.

The ideal candidate will be ready to start quickly and can commit to an initial contract period of at least three months, though we're open to discussing longer-term arrangements for the right fit. If you're excited about working on design tools that transform how people create together, and you're looking for a contract role that offers real autonomy and impact, we'd love to hear from you.

Cloudflare is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our London office on a Contract basis in a Hybrid working arrangement. You'll be working on critical infrastructure that powers millions of websites and applications across the globe, solving complex distributed systems challenges that impact internet performance and security at scale.

In this role, you'll take ownership of significant backend systems, collaborating with experienced engineers to design, build, and maintain services that handle billions of requests daily. Your responsibilities will include architecting scalable solutions, writing production-grade code in languages such as Rust, Go, or C++, and participating in the full software development lifecycle from design through deployment. You'll conduct code reviews, mentor junior developers, and contribute to our engineering culture by sharing knowledge and best practices. Additionally, you'll be expected to troubleshoot production issues, optimize system performance, and work cross-functionally with product and infrastructure teams to deliver features that enhance our platform.

We're looking for candidates with at least seven years of software engineering experience, with demonstrated expertise in backend systems, cloud infrastructure, or distributed computing. You should have strong proficiency in at least one systems programming language and a solid understanding of networking concepts, databases, and API design. Experience with containerization, Kubernetes, or similar orchestration platforms is highly valued. Beyond technical skills, we need someone who communicates effectively, thrives in collaborative environments, and maintains a commitment to code quality and operational excellence.

Cloudflare offers a competitive package including comprehensive health insurance, generous paid time off, and professional development opportunities. Our hybrid model allows you to split your time between our modern London office and remote work, providing flexibility while maintaining strong team cohesion. You'll have access to cutting-edge tools, work on problems that matter, and join a company recognized for its innovation in internet infrastructure and security.

If you're passionate about building systems that power the internet and want to make a tangible impact on web performance and security, we encourage you to apply. Please submit your resume, a cover letter describing your relevant experience, and any portfolio links showcasing your work.

Cloudflare is seeking a talented ML Engineer to join our growing team in Toronto, Canada. We're looking for someone passionate about machine learning and its applications in cybersecurity and performance optimization. This is a full-time position offering a competitive salary of 90,000 - 120,000 based on experience and qualifications.

In this role, you'll work on developing and deploying machine learning models that power our platform's threat detection, DDoS mitigation, and performance optimization features. You'll collaborate with data scientists, software engineers, and product managers to identify opportunities where ML can solve real customer problems at scale. Your work will directly impact the security and performance of millions of websites and applications worldwide.

You should have a strong foundation in machine learning fundamentals with experience building, training, and deploying models in production environments. Proficiency in Python and familiarity with common ML frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch is essential. Experience working with large datasets, implementing data pipelines, and optimizing model performance is crucial. You'll need solid software engineering practices including version control, testing, and code review processes. Knowledge of distributed computing systems and cloud platforms is a plus.

Beyond technical skills, we're looking for someone who communicates clearly, works well in cross-functional teams, and demonstrates curiosity about emerging ML techniques and industry trends. Experience in cybersecurity or network security domains is valuable but not required if you're eager to learn.

Cloudflare offers a comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, professional development opportunities, and a collaborative work environment. We believe in flexible work arrangements and trust our employees to manage their time effectively. You'll have access to cutting-edge tools and infrastructure to support your work, and you'll be part of a company committed to building a better Internet.

If you're excited about applying machine learning to solve complex problems at scale and want to work with a team that values innovation and technical excellence, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now to join Cloudflare's Toronto office and help shape the future of Internet performance and security.

Cloudflare is seeking a Platform Engineer to join our growing team in Toronto, Canada. In this Full-time role, you'll be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the infrastructure and tools that enable our engineering teams to develop and deploy applications efficiently at scale. You'll work on our internal platforms, CI/CD systems, and developer tooling to improve productivity across the organization while ensuring reliability and security standards are met.

Your responsibilities will include architecting and implementing platform solutions that abstract complexity away from application engineers, managing containerized workloads and orchestration systems, and continuously improving our deployment pipelines and monitoring infrastructure. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand their needs and translate those into scalable platform features. Additionally, you'll be involved in troubleshooting production issues, optimizing system performance, and documenting best practices for internal teams.

We're looking for someone with strong experience in cloud infrastructure, ideally with hands-on knowledge of Kubernetes, Docker, or similar container technologies. You should have a solid foundation in Linux systems administration and networking concepts. Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform or Ansible is highly valued. We need someone who can write clear, maintainable code in languages such as Go, Python, or similar, and who has a demonstrated ability to think systematically about complex problems. A background working in high-traffic environments or with distributed systems is beneficial. You should possess excellent communication skills and be comfortable working in collaborative team environments.

Cloudflare offers competitive compensation with a salary range of 90,000 - 120,000, commensurate with experience. Beyond salary, we provide comprehensive health and wellness benefits, professional development opportunities, and a collaborative culture that values innovation and ownership. You'll have access to industry-leading tools and the chance to work on problems that impact millions of internet users worldwide. We're committed to fostering diversity and creating an inclusive workplace where every team member can thrive.

If you're passionate about building robust, scalable systems and want to contribute to a platform that powers the modern internet, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now to join Cloudflare's Platform Engineering team in Toronto.

Sentry is seeking a talented Software Engineer for an Internship position to join our growing engineering team. This is a Remote (US only) opportunity perfect for students or recent graduates looking to gain real-world experience in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. We're looking for someone passionate about building reliable software and eager to contribute to a platform used by millions of developers worldwide.

In this role, you'll work alongside experienced engineers on meaningful projects that directly impact our product. Your responsibilities will include writing clean, maintainable code, participating in code reviews, debugging complex issues, and contributing to the design and implementation of new features. You'll have the opportunity to work across our full stack, whether that's backend services built in Python and Go, frontend components in React, or infrastructure tooling. We value learning and growth, so you'll receive mentorship from senior engineers who are invested in your development.

We're looking for someone with a solid foundation in computer science fundamentals and experience with at least one programming language. You should be comfortable with version control systems like Git, have familiarity with relational databases, and demonstrate strong problem-solving skills. While not required, experience with web development, API design, or cloud platforms is a plus. Most importantly, we're looking for someone who's curious, communicative, and genuinely interested in software engineering as a career.

The compensation for this Internship position ranges from 90,000 - 120,000, depending on experience and qualifications. Beyond salary, we offer comprehensive benefits including health insurance, unlimited PTO, professional development opportunities, and access to our learning resources. You'll work in a supportive environment where your ideas matter and your contributions are recognized.

This Remote (US only) internship is an excellent opportunity to build your portfolio, expand your network, and gain exposure to enterprise-scale software development. You'll work with modern technologies and best practices while contributing to a product that developers rely on daily. If you're excited about this opportunity and meet the qualifications, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today to join the Sentry team and take the next step in your engineering career.

Snowflake is seeking a talented Software Engineer to join our growing engineering team in Dublin, Ireland. This is a full-time position offering a competitive salary of $180k+ based on experience and qualifications. We're looking for someone who is passionate about building scalable, cloud-native solutions and thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

In this role, you will contribute to the development and enhancement of Snowflake's cloud data platform, working alongside experienced engineers to design and implement features that serve thousands of customers worldwide. You'll be responsible for writing clean, maintainable code, participating in code reviews, and collaborating with product managers and architects to translate requirements into robust technical solutions. Your work will directly impact how enterprises store, process, and analyze their data at scale. You'll also have opportunities to mentor junior team members and help shape our engineering culture as we continue to expand our Dublin operations.

We're looking for candidates with strong fundamentals in software engineering, including proficiency in languages such as Java, C++, or Python. You should have experience with distributed systems, database internals, or cloud computing, though we're willing to invest in smart engineers who demonstrate the ability to learn quickly. A solid understanding of software design principles, APIs, and modern development practices is essential. Experience with SQL and familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS or Azure is a plus. Beyond technical skills, we value clear communication, intellectual curiosity, and a collaborative mindset.

Snowflake offers comprehensive benefits including competitive health insurance, professional development opportunities, flexible working arrangements, and access to learning resources to help you grow your career. We foster an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives are valued and innovation is encouraged. Our Dublin office provides a vibrant tech community with strong connections to the broader European market.

If you're excited about the opportunity to work on cutting-edge data platform technology and grow with a rapidly scaling company, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now with your resume and a brief cover letter describing your experience and what attracts you to this opportunity.

Anthropic is seeking a Senior Software Engineer for an Internship position based in Dublin, Ireland. This is an exceptional opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI safety and research projects alongside world-class engineers and researchers who are pushing the boundaries of what's possible in artificial intelligence.

In this role, you'll contribute to the development and deployment of Claude, our state-of-the-art AI assistant, while gaining hands-on experience with large-scale machine learning systems. You'll work on critical infrastructure, optimization challenges, and novel approaches to AI safety. Your responsibilities will include designing and implementing robust software solutions, collaborating with research teams to translate novel ideas into production systems, debugging complex distributed systems, and contributing to our codebase across multiple domains including inference optimization, safety mechanisms, and deployment infrastructure.

We're looking for candidates with strong fundamentals in computer science, demonstrated proficiency in languages like Python, C++, or Rust, and the ability to learn quickly in a fast-paced environment. While this is an internship position, we expect Senior Software Engineer-level problem-solving abilities and independence. You should have experience with systems programming, familiarity with machine learning concepts, and a track record of shipping quality code. Experience with cloud infrastructure, containerization, or distributed systems is highly valued.

What makes this opportunity unique is the chance to work on problems that directly impact AI safety and alignment. You'll be embedded in a company where engineering excellence meets cutting-edge research, where your contributions matter immediately, and where you'll learn from some of the most talented engineers in the field. Our Dublin, Ireland office is part of a growing team focused on making AI systems more reliable, interpretable, and beneficial.

We offer competitive benefits including professional development opportunities, collaborative work environment, and the chance to publish and present your work. You'll have access to significant computational resources and mentorship from senior team members. This internship is structured to provide meaningful technical challenges while supporting your growth as an engineer.

If you're passionate about AI safety, excel at solving hard technical problems, and want to contribute to research that matters, we'd love to hear from you. Apply with your resume, portfolio, or GitHub profile, and tell us about projects you're proud of.

Plaid is seeking a talented Frontend Engineer to join our team on a Contract basis. This is a Remote position ideal for an experienced developer ready to make an immediate impact on our platform. We're looking for someone who can contribute to building intuitive, performant user interfaces that millions rely on daily.

In this role, you'll work on modern web applications using React, TypeScript, and contemporary frontend technologies. Your responsibilities will include designing and implementing new user-facing features, optimizing application performance, collaborating with product and design teams to translate requirements into elegant solutions, and maintaining code quality through testing and peer reviews. You'll also participate in architectural discussions and help establish best practices for frontend development across the organization.

We're looking for candidates with strong proficiency in JavaScript or TypeScript and demonstrable experience building scalable web applications. You should be comfortable working in a fast-paced environment, have excellent problem-solving skills, and be passionate about creating great user experiences. Experience with state management, API integration, and browser optimization is important. We value candidates who care about code quality, can communicate effectively with cross-functional teams, and have a track record of shipping features in production environments.

The ideal candidate will have at least three years of professional frontend development experience and familiarity with modern development workflows and tools. Experience with financial technology or fintech products is a plus but not required. You should be self-motivated, able to work independently in a remote setting, and comfortable jumping into an established codebase while also contributing to improvements in our development processes.

Plaid offers competitive compensation for this contract position, with a salary range of 90,000 - 120,000 depending on experience and qualifications. Beyond compensation, you'll have the opportunity to work with a talented engineering team, contribute to products used by major financial institutions, and develop skills across the full spectrum of modern frontend development. This contract role offers flexibility while providing meaningful work on infrastructure that powers financial connectivity. If you're a skilled frontend engineer ready to take on new challenges and grow your expertise, we'd love to hear from you.

Anthropic is seeking an Engineering Manager to join our Toronto, Canada office and lead a talented team of engineers building safe, beneficial AI systems. This is a full-time position offering a competitive salary of $150,000 - $200,000 based on experience and qualifications.

As Engineering Manager, you will oversee a team of 4-8 software engineers working on core infrastructure and research implementation projects. You'll be responsible for hiring and developing world-class talent, establishing team processes and best practices, and ensuring delivery of high-impact projects on schedule. This role requires balancing technical depth with people leadership, as you'll be expected to remain hands-on with architectural decisions while mentoring engineers at various career stages. You'll work cross-functionally with research scientists, product managers, and other engineering teams to translate research insights into robust, scalable systems.

We're looking for candidates with at least 5 years of software engineering experience and 2+ years of team leadership or mentorship experience. You should have a strong track record of shipping production systems, ideally in high-scale environments or research settings. Experience with machine learning infrastructure, cloud platforms (AWS, GCP), or distributed systems is valuable. Most importantly, we seek someone who is genuinely excited about AI safety and building responsible AI systems, with excellent communication skills and a collaborative leadership style.

Anthropic offers a comprehensive benefits package including competitive health and wellness coverage, professional development budgets, flexible work arrangements, and stock options. We foster a culture of intellectual curiosity and ethical responsibility, where engineers are encouraged to think deeply about the implications of their work. Our Toronto office provides a collaborative environment with access to world-class research and engineering teams.

If you're an experienced engineer ready to step into a leadership role and contribute to making AI systems more safe and beneficial, we'd love to hear from you. Please submit your resume, a cover letter describing your relevant experience, and answers to our technical screening questions. We review applications on a rolling basis and move quickly for strong candidates.

Anthropic is seeking a talented Backend Engineer to join our team on a contract basis. This is a remote opportunity for an experienced engineer ready to make an impact on cutting-edge AI safety and research infrastructure.

You'll be responsible for designing and implementing robust backend systems that support our AI research and deployment operations. Your work will involve building scalable APIs, managing data pipelines, and ensuring system reliability at scale. You'll collaborate closely with our research team to understand their computational needs and translate those into elegant backend solutions. This includes optimizing database performance, implementing caching strategies, and maintaining the infrastructure that powers our most critical systems. You'll also participate in code reviews, contribute to architectural decisions, and help establish best practices for backend development across the organization.

We're looking for candidates with strong fundamentals in backend engineering and a track record of shipping production systems. You should have substantial experience with Python or similar languages, relational databases, and cloud infrastructure platforms. Experience with distributed systems, containerization, and CI/CD pipelines is important. We value engineers who think carefully about system design, can balance quick iteration with long-term maintainability, and communicate clearly about technical tradeoffs. A background in machine learning infrastructure or AI systems is a plus but not required—we care more about your engineering fundamentals and ability to learn quickly in a specialized domain.

Beyond technical skills, we're looking for someone who's genuinely interested in AI safety and aligned with our mission. You should be comfortable working independently and taking ownership of projects, while also being collaborative and receptive to feedback. The ability to thrive in a fast-moving research environment where requirements evolve is essential.

This contract position offers the flexibility of remote work, allowing you to contribute to important research from wherever you're based. You'll work with a team of talented engineers and researchers pushing the boundaries of AI capabilities and safety. If you're excited about building the infrastructure that enables frontier AI research and want to contribute to making AI systems more reliable and interpretable, we'd love to hear from you.

Stripe is seeking a Site Reliability Engineer for our London office on a hybrid basis. This is a part-time position ideal for someone looking to contribute meaningfully to our infrastructure while maintaining flexibility in their work schedule.

You'll be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the systems that keep Stripe's platform reliable and performant at scale. Your day-to-day work will involve monitoring our production infrastructure, responding to incidents, and implementing solutions that prevent future outages. You'll collaborate closely with our engineering teams to identify bottlenecks, optimize our systems, and ensure we're meeting our reliability targets. This includes writing code to automate operational tasks, managing our cloud infrastructure, and documenting best practices that help the entire organization operate more effectively.

We're looking for someone with solid experience in systems administration, cloud platforms, and infrastructure automation. You should be comfortable with at least one major programming language and have hands-on experience with containerization technologies like Docker and Kubernetes. Experience with monitoring tools, log aggregation systems, and incident management platforms is valuable. Beyond technical skills, we need someone who communicates clearly, enjoys mentoring others, and can remain calm under pressure when production incidents occur.

You'll bring a strong understanding of Linux systems, networking fundamentals, and database concepts to the role. Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools like Terraform is highly desirable. Most importantly, you should have a genuine passion for reliability engineering and understand how to balance innovation with stability.

Stripe offers competitive benefits including comprehensive health coverage, professional development opportunities, and access to our learning platforms. While we maintain a hybrid setup for this London-based role, you'll have flexibility to work from our office and home as your schedule permits. You'll work alongside talented engineers who are genuinely invested in solving complex infrastructure challenges. We foster an environment where reliability engineering is valued as a critical discipline, and your contributions will directly impact millions of transactions processed daily across our platform.

If you're a part-time Site Reliability Engineer candidate ready to help us build more reliable systems at Stripe, we'd love to hear from you.

Sentry is seeking a Full-Stack Engineer to join our growing team in Dublin, Ireland. In this full-time role, you'll work on building and maintaining the systems that help millions of developers catch and fix bugs before they impact users.

You'll be responsible for developing new features across our entire stack, from backend services written in Python and Go to our React-based frontend applications. This means you'll contribute to both the core infrastructure that powers error tracking and the user-facing interfaces that developers interact with daily. You'll participate in code reviews, collaborate with product and design teams to understand requirements, and help shape the technical direction of our platform. Your work will directly impact how developers worldwide monitor and improve their applications.

We're looking for someone with strong fundamentals in full-stack development. You should have solid experience with modern web technologies and be comfortable working in multiple programming languages. Experience with cloud platforms, databases, and distributed systems is valuable. Beyond technical skills, we need someone who communicates clearly, enjoys problem-solving collaboratively, and cares about code quality and user experience. You don't need to be an expert in every technology we use, but you should be genuinely interested in learning and growing as an engineer.

What makes this opportunity compelling is the intersection of technical challenges and real-world impact. Our platform processes billions of events monthly, presenting interesting scalability problems. You'll work with experienced engineers who are passionate about building robust systems and take pride in their craft. The Dublin office provides a vibrant tech community with strong connections to the broader European tech scene.

We offer competitive compensation, comprehensive health and wellness benefits, professional development budgets, and flexible working arrangements. You'll have access to modern tools and infrastructure, and we encourage continuous learning through conferences, training, and certifications. We maintain a collaborative culture where your ideas matter and where we support each other's growth.

If you're a full-stack engineer who enjoys end-to-end ownership of features and wants to solve meaningful technical problems at scale, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now and let's talk about how you can contribute to Sentry's mission.

DeepMind is seeking a Research Engineer to join our San Francisco, CA office on a contract basis. This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of artificial intelligence research, contributing to groundbreaking projects that advance the field of machine learning and AI safety.

In this role, you will collaborate with world-class researchers and engineers to design, implement, and optimize novel algorithms and systems. Your responsibilities will include developing software tools and frameworks that enable cutting-edge research, debugging complex distributed systems, and contributing to the technical infrastructure that supports our research initiatives. You'll work closely with research scientists to transform theoretical concepts into practical implementations, while also maintaining the code quality and documentation standards necessary for reproducible research. This position requires someone who can bridge the gap between research ideas and production-ready systems, with strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

We're looking for candidates with a solid foundation in computer science or related fields, demonstrated experience with Python or similar languages, and familiarity with machine learning frameworks. You should have a track record of writing clean, maintainable code and experience working in collaborative research environments. Knowledge of distributed computing, cloud infrastructure, or systems programming is highly valued. A background in mathematics or physics is beneficial though not strictly required. We seek individuals who are self-motivated, intellectually curious, and excited about the potential of AI to solve important problems.

The salary range for this contract position is 70,000 - 95,000 annually, commensurate with experience and qualifications. We offer competitive benefits including health insurance coverage, retirement plan contributions, and access to professional development resources. Contract employees enjoy flexibility and the opportunity to work on diverse projects while maintaining a strong connection to our research community.

If you're passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI research and want to make a tangible impact, we encourage you to apply. Please submit your resume, a brief cover letter describing your relevant experience, and links to any code samples or research projects you'd like to highlight. We review applications on a rolling basis and look forward to hearing from talented engineers interested in joining DeepMind.

Supabase is seeking a talented Backend Engineer to join our growing team in New York, NY. We're building the open-source Firebase alternative, and we need experienced engineers to help us scale our platform and serve thousands of developers worldwide.

In this Full-time role, you'll be responsible for designing and implementing robust backend systems that power our PostgreSQL-based platform. You'll work on critical infrastructure components including authentication systems, real-time APIs, and database management features. Your responsibilities will include writing clean, maintainable code, participating in code reviews, optimizing database queries and API performance, and collaborating with product and frontend teams to deliver features that developers love. You'll also contribute to our open-source projects and help shape the technical direction of our platform.

We're looking for someone with strong experience in backend development, ideally with expertise in TypeScript or similar modern languages. You should have a solid understanding of relational databases, REST APIs, and microservices architecture. Experience with PostgreSQL is a plus, as it's central to everything we do. We value engineers who are passionate about developer experience, can think critically about system design, and enjoy working in a fast-paced startup environment. You should be comfortable with cloud platforms like AWS or Google Cloud, and have experience with Docker and containerization.

Beyond technical skills, we're looking for someone who communicates well, takes ownership of their work, and thrives in collaborative environments. Open-source contributions and a portfolio of projects you're proud of are great indicators of the kind of engineer we want on our team.

Supabase offers a Competitive salary commensurate with experience and qualifications. Our benefits package includes health insurance, equity options, professional development opportunities, and the flexibility to work with cutting-edge technologies. We're a remote-first company, though this New York-based position offers opportunities for in-person collaboration with team members. We foster an inclusive, supportive culture where your ideas are valued and your growth is prioritized. If you're excited about building the future of backend infrastructure and want to work alongside talented engineers solving challenging problems, we'd love to hear from you.
